title: building AI trading bot but keeping strategy private from hosting provider hardware options
author: Nervous_Web_9214
selftext:
I developed a profitable trading strategy using machine learning and want to automate with a bot but concerned about hosting. If I use aws or gcp technically their staff could access my strategy or monitor my trades. I've spent years developing this and dont want it leaked or front run, bare metal server is an option but then I'm responsible for everything as a solo trader which is a lot.

What are other traders using for hosting sensitive algorithms? has to be a middle ground between trust the cloud completely and run your own datacenter.

comments.author: virtuallynudebot
comments.body: your paranoia is justified, theres documented cases of cloud providers employees accessing customer workloads the encryption paradox is real but there's some through trusted execution environments, basically special features that create a secure enclave where code runs isolated from everything else even root access on the server can't peek into the enclave. I trade algorithmically and went through this decision, looked at bare metal colocation which is like $400-800/month plus you handle all maintenance and security. I found Phala because its designed for this things you want computation power without trusting the provider. Your strategy code and your api keys never exist in accessible form outside the enclave. I can verify my bot is running in genuine secure hardware and nobody including phala can access my strategy or see my trades, I've been running it for 8 months with zero issues.

comments.author: Adventurous_Chart360
comments.body:
I run my predictions locally and publish recommendations to a publicly hosted website  
[https://tradebotengine.com/#recommendations](https://tradebotengine.com/#recommendations)

comments.author: Flimsy_Hat_7326
comments.body: this is a real concern, heard stories of strategies being reverse engineered by hosting providers

comments.author: Much_Lingonberry2839
comments.body: unless youre making serious money the paranoia might not be worth it, cloud providers have bigger fish to fry
